proficiencyLevels.name: Absolute Beginner
proficiencyLevels.description: Start from zero — learn the alphabet, basic greetings, and survival phrases

sections.title: Everyday Basics
sections.description: Learn essential words and phrases for daily life

units.title: Greetings & Introductions
objectives: Say hello and goodbye in formal and informal settings

newVocabulary.exampleSentence: Guten Morgen! Wie geht es Ihnen?

units.grammarFocus: Basic sentence structure: Subject + Verb (Ich heiße ...)
units.culturalNote: Germans distinguish between formal (Sie) and informal (du) address. Use 'Sie' with strangers and 'du' with friends.

exercises.instruction: Listen and read the sentence. Tap each word to see its meaning.

content.translation: Good day, my name is Anna.

exercises.instruction: Complete the conversation by choosing the correct response.

dialogue.speaker: app
dialogue.translation: Good day! What is your name?

exercises.instruction: Match the German words with their English meanings.
Arrange the words to form a correct sentence.
Fill in the blank with the correct word.
exercises.hint: Think about how to say 'my name is' in German.

content.text: Ich _____ Maria.

content.question: What does 'Guten Tag' mean?
Ich _____ Thomas. (My name is Thomas.)

exercises.instruction: Read the German text and write what it means in English.

content.prompt: Read the following and write what it means in English:
content.text: Hallo! Ich heiße Maria. Guten Tag!
content.sampleAnswer: Hello! My name is Maria. Good day!

units.title: Numbers 1-20
units.grammarFocus: German number words and their pronunciation patterns
units.culturalNote: Germans count on their fingers starting with the thumb for 'one'.

exercises.instruction: Listen and read the numbers. Tap each word to see its meaning.
Complete the conversation by choosing the correct response.

dialogue.speaker: app
dialogue.translation: How many siblings do you have?

exercises.instruction: Match the German numbers with their English equivalents.
Arrange the words to form a correct sentence.
Fill in the blank with the correct number word.
exercises.hint: The number in parentheses is your clue.

content.text: Ich habe _____ Geschwister. (2)

content.question: What number is 'fünf'?

content.rule: In German, 'haben' (to have) conjugates to 'habe' for 'ich'.
content.question: Ich _____ drei Katzen. (I have three cats.)

exercises.instruction: Read the German text and write what it means in English.

content.prompt: Read the following and write what it means:
content.text: Ich habe fünf Bücher und drei Stifte.
content.sampleAnswer: I have five books and three pens.

units.title: Colors & Descriptions
units.grammarFocus: Adjective placement: predicative (Das Auto ist rot) vs. attributive (das rote Auto)

exercises.instruction: Listen and read. Tap each word to see its meaning.
Match the German colors with their English meanings.
Arrange the words to form a correct sentence.
Fill in the blank with the correct color.

content.text: Der Himmel ist _____.

content.question: What color is 'grün'?

content.rule: In predicative use, German adjectives do not take endings: 'Das Auto ist rot.'
content.question: Das Haus ist _____. (The house is white.)
content.explanation: Predicative adjectives (after 'ist') take no ending in German.

exercises.instruction: Read the German text and write what it means in English.

content.text: Die Katze ist schwarz und weiß.
content.sampleAnswer: The cat is black and white.

units.title: The Alphabet & Pronunciation
objectives: Pronounce all German letters including umlauts (ä, ö, ü) and ß
units.grammarFocus: German umlauts (ä, ö, ü) and the Eszett (ß) — sounds that don't exist in English
units.culturalNote: In Germany, when spelling on the phone, people use city names: A wie Anton, B wie Berlin.

exercises.instruction: Listen to the German alphabet sounds.

content.sentence: A, B, C, D, E, F, G
content.translation: A, B, C, D, E, F, G

exercises.instruction: Match the special characters with their sounds.

content.prompt: A sharp 's' sound, used in words like 'Straße'

exercises.instruction: Fill in the missing umlaut letter.

content.text: Ich m_____chte Kaffee. (I would like coffee.)

content.question: Which of these is NOT a German umlaut?

content.rule: The Eszett (ß) is used after long vowels. 'ss' is used after short vowels.
content.question: Die Stra___e ist lang. (The street is long.)
content.explanation: After the long 'a' sound in 'Straße', we use 'ß'.

content.prompt: Write the German word for 'street' using the correct special character:
content.text: Straße

units.title: Basic Phrases & Politeness
objectives: Use common polite phrases (please, thank you, excuse me)
units.grammarFocus: Formal (Sie) vs. informal (du) forms of address

content.text: Wie _____ es Ihnen?

content.question: What does 'Entschuldigung' mean?

content.rule: 'Wie geht es Ihnen?' uses formal 'Sie'. 'Wie geht es dir?' uses informal 'du'.
content.question: Wie geht es _____? (formal: How are you?)
content.explanation: 'Ihnen' is the formal dative form of 'Sie'.

content.text: Entschuldigung! Wie geht es Ihnen? Mir geht es gut, danke.
content.sampleAnswer: Excuse me! How are you? I'm fine, thank you.

units.title: Food & Drink Basics
objectives: Name common food and drink items
Say what you like and dislike
units.grammarFocus: Definite articles by gender: der (m), die (f), das (n)
units.culturalNote: Germans traditionally eat bread (Brot) for breakfast and dinner. Lunch is the main hot meal.

dialogue.speaker: app
dialogue.translation: What would you like to drink?

content.text: Ich möchte _____, bitte.

content.question: Which article goes with 'Milch'?

content.rule: German nouns have grammatical gender: der (masculine), die (feminine), das (neuter).
content.question: _____ Kaffee ist heiß. (The coffee is hot.)

content.text: Ich möchte Kaffee und Brot, bitte.
content.sampleAnswer: I would like coffee and bread, please.

units.title: Family Members
units.grammarFocus: Possessive pronoun 'mein' changes with gender: mein Vater (m), meine Mutter (f), mein Kind (n)

content.text: _____ Bruder heißt Thomas.

content.question: What does 'Schwester' mean?

content.rule: 'Mein' changes based on gender: mein (m/n), meine (f/pl).
content.question: _____ Schwester ist nett. (My sister is nice.)

content.text: Meine Familie ist groß. Ich habe zwei Brüder und eine Schwester.
content.sampleAnswer: My family is big. I have two brothers and one sister.

content.sentence: Ich habe einen Hund und eine Katze.
content.translation: I have a dog and a cat.

content.text: Ich habe einen _____.

content.question: What is the plural of 'Tier'?

content.rule: In the accusative case, masculine articles change: der → den, ein → einen.
content.question: Ich habe _____ Hund. (I have a dog.)
content.explanation: 'Hund' is masculine. In the accusative, 'ein' becomes 'einen'.

content.text: Ich habe zwei Hunde und drei Fische.
content.sampleAnswer: I have two dogs and three fish.

units.title: Body Parts
objectives: Use definite articles with body parts
units.grammarFocus: Body parts use definite articles, not possessives: 'Mir tut der Kopf weh' (My head hurts)

content.text: Mir tut _____ Hand weh.

content.question: What does 'Auge' mean?

content.rule: German uses definite articles (not possessives) with body parts in pain expressions.
content.question: Mir tut _____ Kopf weh.
content.explanation: German uses 'der Kopf' with the dative 'mir' instead of 'mein Kopf' in pain expressions.

content.text: Mir tut der Kopf weh und meine Hand tut auch weh.
content.sampleAnswer: My head hurts and my hand also hurts.

units.title: Review & Practice
objectives: Practice greetings, numbers, colors, and family
units.grammarFocus: Review: articles (der/die/das), possessives (mein/meine), verb conjugation (ich heiße, ich habe)

content.sentence: Hallo! Ich heiße Maria. Ich habe zwei Katzen.
content.translation: Hello! My name is Maria. I have two cats.

dialogue.speaker: app
dialogue.translation: Good day! What is your name?

dialogue.speaker: app
dialogue.text: Freut mich! Wie geht es Ihnen?
dialogue.translation: Nice to meet you! How are you?

options.text: Ich heiße Thomas. / Mir geht es gut, danke!

exercises.instruction: Match all the words you've learned.

content.question: How do you say 'Excuse me' in German?

content.rule: Review: articles match gender, possessives change with gender.
content.question: _____ Katze ist schwarz. (The cat is black.)

exercises.instruction: Write a short introduction in English based on this German text.

content.prompt: Read and translate the full paragraph:
content.text: Hallo! Ich heiße Maria. Ich habe eine Schwester und einen Bruder. Meine Katze ist schwarz. Ich trinke gern Kaffee. Auf Wiedersehen!
content.sampleAnswer: Hello! My name is Maria. I have a sister and a brother. My cat is black. I like to drink coffee. Goodbye!

exercises.instruction: Match all the key vocabulary from Section 1.

content.question: Complete: 'Ich _____ Anna und ich habe zwei Katzen.'

exercises.instruction: Write a short self-introduction in English from this German text.

content.text: Guten Tag! Ich heiße Thomas. Ich habe einen Bruder und eine Schwester. Mein Hund ist braun. Ich trinke gern Tee.
content.sampleAnswer: Good day! My name is Thomas. I have a brother and a sister. My dog is brown. I like to drink tea.

sections.title: People & Things
sections.description: Learn to talk about family, body parts, animals, food and drink

sections.title: Daily Routines
sections.description: Talk about time, weather, places, and getting around

sections.title: Social Situations
sections.description: Handle ordering at a cafe, shopping, asking directions, and dining out

sections.title: Building Sentences
sections.description: Learn present tense verbs, adjectives, describing people, and review everything

proficiencyLevels.name: Beginner
proficiencyLevels.description: Build on basics — express gratitude, talk about past and future, handle daily situations

sections.title: Past & Future
sections.description: Past tense, talking about experiences, making plans

sections.title: Daily Life
sections.description: Hobbies, sports, housing, work and school

sections.title: Practical Communication
sections.description: Health, complaints, giving advice, phone calls

proficiencyLevels.name: Intermediate
proficiencyLevels.description: Develop fluency — express opinions, handle professional situations, understand culture

proficiencyLevels.name: Advanced
proficiencyLevels.description: Master complex grammar and specialized vocabulary for professional and academic contexts
